String manipulation functions for memory and string operations in C.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares a set of standard string manipulation functions. It includes function prototypes for [`memcpy`](<#memcpy>), [`memset`](<#memset>), [`memmove`](<#memmove>), [`memcmp`](<#memcmp>), [`strcmp`](<#strcmp>), [`strncmp`](<#strncmp>), [`strncpy`](<#strncpy>), and [`strlen`](<#strlen>), which are commonly used for memory and string operations. The file uses include guards to prevent multiple inclusions and ensures compatibility with C++ by using `extern "C"` linkage specification. The functions declared in this header are essential for performing operations such as copying, setting, comparing, and determining the length of strings and memory blocks.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stddef.h`
